tests.scenario: Display at least 3 selectable emotion options during check-in
steps: Log in as a valid user
Open a new check-in
Navigate to the emotion-selection step
Count the number of selectable emotion options shown
tests.expected_result: At least 3 selectable emotion options are displayed.
tests.notes: Reduced the required emotion count to 3 and removed the category-selection assumption because the hybrid requirement only requires at least 3 selectable emotion options during check-in.

tests.scenario: Select 1 emotion option during check-in
steps: Log in as a valid user
Open a new check-in
Navigate to the emotion-selection step
Select 1 displayed emotion option
tests.expected_result: The selected emotion option is accepted by the system and remains selected.
tests.notes: Changed the scenario to verify that one emotion can actually be selected because the hybrid requirement tests selection behavior, not what happens when no category is chosen.

tests.scenario: Display two completed check-ins in chronological order
steps: Log in as a valid user
Create and submit 2 check-ins at different times
Open the history page
tests.expected_result: The 2 completed check-ins are displayed in chronological order by date and time.
tests.notes: Replaced the weekly graph check with chronological history verification because the hybrid requirement requires ordered check-in history, not a graph.

tests.scenario: Display multiple completed check-ins in chronological order after app reload
steps: Log in as a valid user
Create and submit 3 check-ins on different dates
Close and reopen the app
Open the history page
tests.expected_result: All 3 completed check-ins are displayed in chronological order by date and time.
tests.notes: Expanded the history scenario to 3 entries and added app reload to confirm that chronological ordering still holds with more than the minimum number of saved check-ins.

tests.scenario: Complete a standard check-in without entering free-text content
steps: Log in as a valid user
Open a standard check-in
Answer the provided prompts
Leave all free-text fields blank
Submit the check-in
tests.expected_result: The check-in is submitted successfully without any mandatory free-text input.
tests.notes: Removed the old insights-oriented behavior and changed the test to verify that a prompted check-in can be submitted without mandatory free-text input, which is the exact hybrid requirement.

tests.scenario: Submit a prompted check-in when no free-text field is completed
steps: Log in as a valid user
Open a standard check-in
Answer only the required prompted questions
Submit the check-in
tests.expected_result: The system accepts the check-in submission without requiring any free-text journal entry.
tests.notes: Focused the test on submitting prompted responses only because the hybrid requirement is specifically about not requiring a free-text journal entry.

tests.scenario: Generate a summary report for a selected date range with two completed check-ins
steps: Log in as a valid user
Create and submit 2 check-ins inside the target date range
Open the summary report feature
Select the start date and end date that include both check-ins
tests.expected_result: The system displays a report containing the selected start date, the selected end date, and a check-in count of 2.
tests.notes: Replaced the subscription-change scenario with date-range report generation because the hybrid requirement now covers summary reports instead of subscription management.

tests.scenario: Generate a summary report for a selected date range with five completed check-ins
steps: Log in as a valid user
Create and submit 5 check-ins inside the target date range
Open the summary report feature
Select the start date and end date that include all 5 check-ins
tests.expected_result: The system displays a report containing the selected start date, the selected end date, and a check-in count of 5.
tests.notes: Added a second report-generation case with 5 check-ins so the test verifies that the report count changes correctly with a larger dataset.

tests.scenario: Confirm check-in submission within 2 seconds
steps: Log in as a valid user
Open a completed check-in ready for submission
Press the submit button
Measure the time until the confirmation message appears
tests.expected_result: The confirmation message appears within 2 seconds after the submit button is pressed.
tests.notes: Kept the response-time measurement idea but moved it from emotion selection to check-in submission because the hybrid requirement measures confirmation time after pressing submit.

tests.scenario: Confirm check-in submission within 2 seconds after multiple prompt responses
steps: Log in as a valid user
Open a check-in and answer all required prompts
Press the submit button
Measure the time until the confirmation message appears
tests.expected_result: The confirmation message appears within 2 seconds after the submit button is pressed.
tests.notes: Removed the subjective usability check and kept only the measurable 2-second confirmation condition because the hybrid requirement must be fully testable.

tests.scenario: Free user completes a check-in and opens check-in history
steps: Log in as a user without an active paid subscription
Complete and submit 1 check-in
Open the check-in history page
tests.expected_result: The user can submit the check-in successfully and can open the saved check-in in the history page without any payment prompt.
tests.notes: Replaced the generic free-features count check with direct verification of the 3 free-tier functions required in the hybrid requirement: completing a check-in and opening history.

tests.scenario: Free user opens one summary page without payment
steps: Log in as a user without an active paid subscription
Ensure at least 1 check-in already exists for the user
Open the summary page
tests.expected_result: The summary page opens without requiring payment.
tests.notes: Changed the scenario to test access to one summary page without payment because the hybrid requirement explicitly includes summary-page access for unpaid users.

tests.scenario: Display price, billing period, and locked-feature list for one premium plan
steps: Log in as a valid user
Open the pricing page
Locate 1 premium plan listing
tests.expected_result: The premium plan listing displays its price, billing period, and at least 1 locked feature.
tests.notes: Kept the pricing-page scenario but narrowed the verification to the exact required fields: price, billing period, and at least one locked feature.

tests.scenario: Display required pricing details for each premium plan shown on the page
steps: Open the pricing page
Review each premium plan shown on the page
tests.expected_result: Each premium plan shown on the page displays a price, a billing period, and at least 1 locked feature.
tests.notes: Kept the page-review idea but changed the expected result so every displayed premium plan must show the same required details defined in the hybrid requirement.

tests.scenario: Unpaid user selects locked premium insight content from the insights page
steps: Log in as a user without an active paid subscription
Open the insights page
Select locked premium insight content
tests.expected_result: The system displays a lock message and does not display the locked insight content.
tests.notes: Changed the expected behavior from showing a free alternative or summary to blocking locked premium insight content because the hybrid requirement now requires lock enforcement.

tests.scenario: Unpaid user attempts to open locked premium insight content directly
steps: Log in as a user without an active paid subscription
Open a direct link or entry point to locked premium insight content
tests.expected_result: The system displays a lock message and does not display the locked insight content.
tests.notes: Changed the second premium-insight test to verify the same lock behavior through a direct access path so the requirement is tested consistently across entry points.

tests.scenario: Display one instruction block before first use of the mood tracking feature
steps: Log in as a valid user who has never opened the mood tracking feature
Open the mood tracking feature for the first time
tests.expected_result: The system displays 1 instruction block before the user submits data for the mood tracking feature.
tests.notes: Replaced vague 'guidance for specific needs' wording with a measurable first-use instruction-block check because the hybrid requirement requires one instruction block before first use.

tests.scenario: Display one instruction block before first use of another tracking feature
steps: Log in as a valid user who has never opened the sleep tracking feature
Open the sleep tracking feature for the first time
tests.expected_result: The system displays 1 instruction block before the user submits data for the sleep tracking feature.
tests.notes: Kept a second feature-based scenario, but changed it to verify first-use instruction display for another tracking feature so the requirement is not tied to only one feature.

tests.scenario: Submitted check-in remains after closing and reopening the app
steps: Log in as a valid user
Submit 1 check-in
Close the app
Reopen the app
Open the check-in history page
tests.expected_result: The submitted check-in appears in check-in history after the app is reopened.
tests.notes: Replaced the app-rating test with a persistence test because the hybrid requirement now measures whether submitted check-ins remain after closing and reopening the app.

tests.scenario: Multiple submitted check-ins remain after closing and reopening the app
steps: Log in as a valid user
Submit 3 check-ins
Close the app
Reopen the app
Open the check-in history page
tests.expected_result: All 3 submitted check-ins appear in check-in history after the app is reopened.
tests.notes: Changed the second reliability test from rating validation to multi-entry persistence so it checks the same hybrid requirement under a larger saved-data case.

tests.scenario: Display at least three predefined discussion prompts on therapy-preparation page
steps: Log in as a valid user
Open the therapy-preparation page
Count the number of predefined discussion prompts shown
tests.expected_result: At least 3 predefined discussion prompts are displayed.
tests.notes: Removed topic-selection dependency and changed the scenario to count predefined prompts because the hybrid requirement requires at least 3 prompts on the therapy-preparation page.

tests.scenario: Display therapy-preparation prompts without requiring a topic selection
steps: Log in as a valid user
Open the therapy-preparation page
tests.expected_result: At least 3 predefined discussion prompts are displayed on page load.
tests.notes: Changed the negative 'no topic selected' case into a page-load case because the hybrid requirement expects predefined prompts to appear without requiring topic selection first.

tests.scenario: Display at least one resource item for selected support topic anxiety
steps: Log in as a valid user
Open the resources page
Select the support topic anxiety
tests.expected_result: At least 1 resource item associated with anxiety is displayed.
tests.notes: Kept the support-topic structure but made the expected result measurable by requiring at least 1 resource item for the selected topic anxiety.

tests.scenario: Display at least one resource item for selected support topic depression
steps: Log in as a valid user
Open the resources page
Select the support topic depression
tests.expected_result: At least 1 resource item associated with depression is displayed.
tests.notes: Kept the second support-topic case and made it measurable in the same way so the test verifies topic-to-resource behavior for depression as well.

tests.scenario: Display mood entries from the previous 30 days
steps: Log in as a valid user
Ensure the user has at least 2 recorded mood entries within the previous 30 days
Open the mood-history page
tests.expected_result: All recorded mood entries from the previous 30 days are displayed.
tests.notes: Removed the graph requirement and changed the test to verify that all mood entries from the previous 30 days are displayed, which matches the hybrid requirement exactly.

tests.scenario: Exclude mood entries older than 30 days from the displayed history
steps: Log in as a valid user
Ensure the user has 1 recorded mood entry within the previous 30 days and 1 recorded mood entry older than 30 days
Open the mood-history page
tests.expected_result: The entry from the previous 30 days is displayed and the entry older than 30 days is not displayed.
tests.notes: Changed the no-data case into a 30-day boundary test because the hybrid requirement is specifically about which entries are included in the previous 30 days.

tests.scenario: Create and edit one daily reminder
steps: Log in as a valid user
Open reminder settings
Create 1 daily reminder
Change the reminder time
Save the updated reminder
tests.expected_result: The reminder is created successfully and the updated reminder time is saved.
tests.notes: Changed the reminder test from generic reminder availability to create-and-edit behavior because the hybrid requirement explicitly requires creating and editing a daily reminder.

tests.scenario: Disable one existing daily reminder
steps: Log in as a valid user
Open reminder settings
Locate 1 existing daily reminder
Disable the reminder
tests.expected_result: The selected daily reminder is disabled successfully.
tests.notes: Changed the second reminder test to focus on disabling an existing reminder because the hybrid requirement separately requires disable behavior.

tests.scenario: Display the most frequently selected mood option for a valid date range
steps: Log in as a valid user
Create or load at least 7 completed check-ins in a selected date range
Ensure one mood option has the highest selection count in that range
Open the pattern or summary view for that date range
tests.expected_result: The system displays the mood option with the highest selection count in the selected date range.
tests.notes: Replaced the vague 'two patterns or trends' check with verification of the highest-count mood option because the hybrid requirement defines pattern output as the most frequently selected mood option.

tests.scenario: Update the displayed most frequent mood option when the date range changes
steps: Log in as a valid user
Create or load at least 7 completed check-ins in date range A and at least 7 completed check-ins in date range B
Ensure date range A and date range B have different highest-count mood options
Open the pattern or summary view for date range A
Change the selected range to date range B
tests.expected_result: The system displays the highest-count mood option for date range A first, then displays the highest-count mood option for date range B after the range is changed.
tests.notes: Changed the minimal-data trend scenario into a date-range change scenario so the test verifies that the displayed highest-count mood option updates when the selected range changes.
